description: R83 fresh04 timeout-class round 3 of 3 longer-repeat (cohort-B single-outbound closure attempt). Outcome: classification B; closure NOT achieved.

summary.accounting_note: divergence_run_count counts runs (a run is a divergence run iff it carries any of the four phase labels app_pre_post_diverged, app_minimal_diverged, minimal_transport_diverged, bridge_io_diverged); divergence_phase_label_count counts phase-label occurrences across those divergence runs (a single run can carry multiple phase labels). label_counts above is per-occurrence, matching divergence_phase_label_breakdown.

phase_probe_supporting_evidence.note: Phase probe ran in all 3 attempts. The matrix-level result is authoritative; phase data is supporting only.

interpretation: Fresh04 only: 3 matrix runs total; no fresh05, no cohort C, no other fresh nodes, no Hysteria2, no WS/plain-VLESS.

All 3 matrix runs returned status=completed. Run 1 carried the known-taxonomy divergence label app_minimal_diverged (minimal.vless_dial=connection_reset while every other class is timeout); runs 2 and 3 produced the uniform-failure pair probe_io_all_timeout + reality_all_timeout.
matrix-level run_health: run_divergence=1 (run 1), run_same_failure=2 (runs 2 and 3), run_all_ok=0, run_unknown=0.

Classification B per prompt v2 — mixed with 1 known-taxonomy divergence. cohort-B single-outbound closure for fresh04 is NOT achieved; the consecutive timeout-class chain stops at round 2 (R78 round 1, R82 round 2). class_history='[other, timeout, null, timeout, null]' where the trailing null is R83 mixed (no single same_failure_class).

fresh04 transitions from a stable cohort-B same_failure candidate into a cohort-A-style re-evaluation candidate. The divergence label app_minimal_diverged is in the four-element taxonomy, so no new structural divergence; no mid-flight stop was required.
Pre-gate (intake + dry-run + R81 subset_schema_gate with empty violations) all passed; the structural failure path that R80 hit is not the cause of R83's mixed outcome.

BHV 52/56 unchanged. Not sampler/dataplane regression. Not dual-kernel parity completion. Closure scope is strictly fresh04 single-outbound + timeout class; this finding does not extend to cohort B as a whole and does not affect the 6 historical stable same-failure outbounds (HK/JP/UK/US-A-BGP) that are already closed.

r80.phase_probe_class_consistency: phase probe 3/3 runs timeout class; consistent with R78 same-failure(timeout) at network level, but not matrix-level confirmation

fresh04_r73_r78_r80_r82_r83_comparison.assessment: R83 produced 1 known-taxonomy divergence (app_minimal_diverged) + 2 same_failure (timeout). cohort-B single-outbound closure for fresh04 is NOT achieved at R83 because the consecutive timeout-class chain stops at round 2 (R78, R82). class_history is ['other', 'timeout', null, 'timeout', null] where the trailing null is R83 mixed (no single same_failure_class). fresh04 transitions from a stable cohort-B same_failure candidate into a cohort-A-style re-evaluation candidate. No new structural divergence; the divergence label is in the four-element taxonomy.

closure_status.chain_broken_reason: R83 produced 1 run_divergence (app_minimal_diverged) + 2 run_same_failure (timeout); not 3/3 same_failure(timeout). The timeout-class longer-repeat chain therefore stops at round 2 (R78, R82) and does not extend to round 3.

classification.label: mixed: 1 known-taxonomy divergence (app_minimal_diverged) + 2 same_failure (timeout); cohort-B single-outbound closure NOT achieved for fresh04
classification.rationale: Run 1 carried the known-taxonomy divergence label app_minimal_diverged (asymmetry between app and minimal probes at the vless_dial layer; minimal.vless_dial=connection_reset while every other class is timeout). Runs 2 and 3 produced the uniform-failure pair probe_io_all_timeout + reality_all_timeout (run_same_failure with class=timeout). The mix of 1 run_divergence + 2 run_same_failure puts R83 in the B branch per prompt v2: cohort-B single-outbound closure for fresh04 is NOT achieved because the consecutive timeout-class chain breaks here at round 2 (R78 round 1, R82 round 2, R83 mixed). fresh04 transitions from a stable cohort-B same_failure candidate into a cohort-A-style re-evaluation candidate. No new structural divergence (the divergence label is in the four-element taxonomy).
classification.next_handling: Do NOT auto-extend R83. fresh04's saga has shifted from stable cohort-B same_failure into a cohort-A-style re-evaluation candidate. Future authorized rounds against fresh04 should treat it as a divergence-carrier candidate (R76 cohort A semantics), not a same_failure carrier. cohort C round-2 (fresh01/09/15 ×3) and the 6 R73 unselected recovery nodes are independent lines and remain unaffected.

redaction_notes: Evidence uses only neutral outbound key fresh04.
Raw tag/server/uuid/public_key/short_id/path/header/server_name/password material remains only in local /tmp inputs and is not committed.
subset_schema_gate.violations is committed only because it is the empty array; the gate's structural redaction (violations carry only path/field/reason) is preserved.
